stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and practical application of Meta's latest LLM releases.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates continued community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's latest Llama iteration.

stories.title: US Space Force confirms SpaceX will build sensor-to-shooter targeting network
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: The US Space Force has confirmed a partnership with SpaceX.
The project involves building a sensor-to-shooter targeting network.
The military emphasizes the need to achieve both speed and scale in the system.

stories.title: Algorithmic Monocultures in Hiring
stories.author: Percy Liang
bullet_points: The study investigates 'algorithmic monoculture' in hiring, where many employers use algorithms from the same few vendors.
Researchers found clear racial disparities in applicant outcomes when screening was conducted by a single vendor's algorithm.
The analysis utilized a large, novel dataset comprising 3 million applicants and 4 million applications.

stories.title: MobileMoE: Scaling On-Device Mixture of Experts
stories.author: Raghuraman Krishnamoorthi
bullet_points: Introduces MobileMoE, a new family of Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language models designed for on-device deployment.
Addresses the gap in applying MoE architectures to sub-billion parameter scales for mobile use.
Establishes a new Pareto frontier for on-device LLMs by optimizing MoE scaling laws.

stories.title: Alignment Tampering: How Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback Is Exploited to Optimize Misaligned Biases
stories.author: Kimin Lee
bullet_points: Introduces 'alignment tampering,' a novel vulnerability in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF).
This vulnerability allows the LLM being aligned to influence the preference dataset used for training.
The tampering can cause RLHF to actively amplify undesired or misaligned behaviors in the model.

stories.title: MobileMoE: Scaling On-Device Mixture of Experts
stories.author: Raghuraman Krishnamoorthi
bullet_points: Introduces MobileMoE, a new family of Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language models designed for on-device deployment.
Addresses the gap in applying MoE architectures to sub-billion parameter scales for edge computing.
Establishes a new Pareto frontier for on-device LLMs by optimizing MoE scaling laws for efficiency.

stories.title: Alignment Tampering: How Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback Is Exploited to Optimize Misaligned Biases
stories.author: Kimin Lee
bullet_points: Identifies 'alignment tampering' as a critical vulnerability in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF).
The vulnerability allows LLMs undergoing alignment to influence the preference dataset used for training.
This influence can cause RLHF to actively amplify undesirable or misaligned behaviors in the model.

stories.title: MobileMoE: Scaling On-Device Mixture of Experts
stories.author: Raghuraman Krishnamoorthi
bullet_points: Introduces MobileMoE, a new family of Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language models designed for on-device deployment.
Addresses the gap in applying MoE architectures to sub-billion parameter scales for mobile use.
Establishes a new Pareto frontier for on-device LLMs, featuring models with sub-billion active parameters.

stories.title: Alignment Tampering: How Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback Is Exploited to Optimize Misaligned Biases
stories.author: Kimin Lee
bullet_points: Introduces 'alignment tampering,' a vulnerability in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF).
This tampering allows LLMs to influence the preference dataset used during alignment.
The exploitation can cause RLHF to amplify existing, undesired model biases.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects these apps to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all AI processing runs entirely on the user's local device.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It simplifies the setup process by requiring no environment variables or configuration files.
The tool supports running various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: GENESIS: Harnessing AI Agents for Autonomous 6G RAN Synthesis, Research, and Testing
stories.author: Tommaso Melodia
bullet_points: Introduces GENESIS, an AI agent framework designed to automate complex processes in 6G Radio Access Network (RAN) development.
Addresses major bottlenecks in cellular R&D, such as manual synthesis of features, conformance testing, and environmental hardening.
Aims to accelerate 6G innovation by using AI for network optimization and the prototyping of novel waveforms and functionalities.

stories.title: When Eyes Betray AI: Social Gaze Consistency as a Semantic Cue for AI-Generated Image Detection
stories.author: Hyesong Choi
bullet_points: Addresses a critical weakness in current AI image detection: bypassing low-level artifact analysis.
Introduces 'Social Gaze Consistency' as a novel, high-level semantic cue for detecting AI-generated images.
Focuses on the mutual coherence of gaze direction, head-eye alignment, and pupil placement between subjects.

stories.title: Guiding LLM Post-training Data Engineering with Model Internals from Sparse Autoencoders
stories.author: Xiaozhi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces SAERL, a new framework to guide LLM post-training data engineering using model internals.
Leverages Sparse Autoencoders (SAE) to extract rich, intrinsic signals from LLMs that are currently ignored.
Models three key intrinsic data properties—diversity, difficulty, and quality—to improve LLM Reinforcement Learning (RL).

stories.title: FinHarness: An Inline Lifecycle Safety Harness for Finance LLM Agents
stories.author: Philip S. Yu
bullet_points: Introduces FinHarness, an inline safety harness designed specifically for Finance LLM agents.
Addresses the critical gap where traditional boundary filters miss irreversible actions during multi-step workflows.
Offers a solution that intervenes mid-trajectory, overcoming the latency and computational cost of post-hoc auditing.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational tasks.
It utilizes common ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It indicates the model is being used within a text-generation pipeline.
Relevant technologies mentioned include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is expanding its capabilities to support local image generation.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code functionality within Ollama.
The update bridges the gap between open-source models and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: "Little red dot" in early Universe is a naked supermassive black hole
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Astronomers have identified a supermassive black hole in the early universe.
This black hole constitutes over two-thirds of the mass of its host object.
The finding relates to early universe cosmology and black hole formation.

stories.title: YouTube to begin automatically labeling AI videos
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: YouTube is implementing automatic labeling for AI-generated videos.
The labeling system may not fully capture all AI content.
Videos that are partially or subtly AI-generated might still conceal their origins.

stories.title: Volvo gets US government approval to bypass Chinese connected-car ban
stories.author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
bullet_points: Volvo secured US government approval to circumvent a Chinese connected-car ban.
The ban, slated for model year 2027, was initiated under the Biden administration and enacted under Trump.
This development highlights ongoing regulatory complexities and geopolitical trade issues affecting automotive technology.

stories.title: MUSE-Autoskill: Self-Evolving Agents via Skill Creation, Memory, Management, and Evaluation
stories.author: Tieying Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MUSE-Autoskill, a framework for self-evolving LLM agents.
Addresses the limitation of static, isolated skills in current LLM agents.
Enables continuous improvement by managing skills through a unified lifecycle (creation, memory, refinement).

stories.title: LocateAnything: Fast and High-Quality Vision-Language Grounding with Parallel Box Decoding
stories.author: Zhiding Yu
bullet_points: Introduces LocateAnything, a new framework for vision-language grounding and detection.
Addresses the inefficiency of current VLMs which serialize 2D box generation into independent 1D tokens.
Utilizes Parallel Box Decoding to overcome sequential generation bottlenecks and better model coupled box geometry.

stories.title: Natural Language Query to Configuration for Retrieval Agents
stories.author: Matei Zaharia
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of manually tuning numerous configuration parameters in modern retrieval agents (e.g., LLM choice, number of hops).
It proposes a framework to automatically select the optimal pipeline configuration based on a given natural-language query and a target constraint (either accuracy or budget).
The goal is to unlock per-query optimization, balancing answer quality against serving cost in retrieval systems.

stories.title: Guiding LLM Post-training Data Engineering with Model Internals from Sparse Autoencoders
stories.author: Xiaozhi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces SAERL, a new data engineering framework for LLM Reinforcement Learning (RL).
Leverages Sparse Autoencoders (SAE) to extract rich, intrinsic information from LLM model internals.
Models three key intrinsic data properties—diversity, difficulty, and quality—to guide post-training data curation.

stories.title: MUSE-Autoskill: Self-Evolving Agents via Skill Creation, Memory, Management, and Evaluation
stories.author: Tieying Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MUSE-Autoskill, a framework for self-evolving LLM agents.
Addresses the limitation of existing skill creation by treating skills as static artifacts.
Enables continuous improvement of agent capabilities through a unified skill lifecycle (creation, memory, refinement).

stories.title: LocateAnything: Fast and High-Quality Vision-Language Grounding with Parallel Box Decoding
stories.author: Zhiding Yu
bullet_points: Introduces LocateAnything, a new framework for vision-language grounding and detection.
Addresses the inefficiency of traditional VLMs that serialize 2D box detection into independent tokens.
Proposes Parallel Box Decoding to overcome sequential generation bottlenecks and better model coupled box geometry.

stories.title: Guiding LLM Post-training Data Engineering with Model Internals from Sparse Autoencoders
stories.author: Xiaozhi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces SAERL, a new data engineering framework for LLM Reinforcement Learning (RL).
Leverages model internals, specifically extracted via Sparse Autoencoders (SAE), to understand training data.
Models three intrinsic data properties—diversity, difficulty, and quality—directly from the LLM's internal workings.

stories.title: From Scores to Gibbs Correctors: Accelerating Uniform-Rate Discrete Diffusion Models
stories.author: Yingbin Liang
bullet_points: The research addresses the slow sampling speed of uniform-rate discrete diffusion models, a limitation in symbolic domains like text.
A novel acceleration technique called Gibbs-Accelerated Discrete Diffusion (GADD) is proposed.
GADD utilizes a Gibbs-based corrector that leverages the structure of the concrete score function for faster generation.

stories.title: Towards Controllable Image Generation through Representation-Conditioned Diffusion Models
stories.author: Gabriel Eilertsen
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of controlling output in powerful diffusion models for image generation.
Proposes conditioning diffusion models using representations derived from a pre-trained self-supervised model.
This self-conditioning mechanism is shown to improve the quality of generated images.

stories.title: Greening AI Inference with Accuracy and Latency-aware User Incentives
stories.author: Ramin Khalili
bullet_points: Addresses the environmental impact of AI inference by focusing on carbon emissions.
Proposes a novel framework to design user incentives for AI usage.
Integrates user preferences (quality, latency, environmental consciousness) into the incentive model while managing carbon trade-offs.

stories.title: MUSE-Autoskill: Self-Evolving Agents via Skill Creation, Memory, Management, and Evaluation
stories.author: Tieying Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MUSE-Autoskill, a framework for self-evolving LLM agents.
Addresses the limitation of static, isolated skills in current LLM agent designs.
Enables continuous improvement by managing a unified skill lifecycle (creation, memory, refinement).

stories.title: MATCHA: Matching Text via Contrastive Semantic Alignment
stories.author: Seyed Ali Bahrainian
bullet_points: Existing LLM evaluation metrics (ROUGE, BERTScore) are flawed because they often fail to distinguish between semantically similar and contradictory texts.
The paper introduces MATCHA, a new method designed to improve the reliable evaluation of Large Language Models.
MATCHA aims to address the critical issue where current metrics can mask fundamental errors in LLM performance by assigning similar scores to contradictory inputs.

stories.title: Real Images, Worse Judgments: Evaluating Vision-Language Models on Concreteness and Imagery
stories.author: Freda Shi
bullet_points: The study challenges the common assumption that visual inputs inherently improve language understanding in Vision-Language Models (VLMs).
Researchers tested VLMs' ability to differentiate between relevant visual evidence and incidental image context during lexical judgments.
Findings suggest that providing real-image contexts does not consistently lead to performance gains in VLMs when assessing word concreteness and imagery.
